questions.text: Which of the following is a vector quantity?
questions.explanation: Velocity has both magnitude and direction, making it a vector quantity. Speed, distance, and time are scalars.

questions.text: The resultant of two perpendicular vectors A and B is:
questions.explanation: For perpendicular vectors, the magnitude of resultant R = √(A² + B²) using Pythagoras theorem.

questions.text: Projectile motion is an example of two-dimensional motion.
questions.explanation: Projectile motion occurs in a vertical plane with both horizontal and vertical components, making it 2D motion.

questions.text: The maximum height of a projectile is given by ________.
questions.explanation: Maximum height H = (v0²sin²θ)/(2g), where v0 is initial velocity, θ is angle of projection, g is acceleration due to gravity.
